name: Elbmesh Plan Implementation Slice
title: Plan an Elbmesh Implementation Slice
summary: Define the next smallest useful Elbmesh implementation slice, task card, acceptance criteria, tests-first plan, non-goals, docs updates, and verification commands.
description: Use this task to coordinate the test-first loop inside the active phase. Use the phase coordination task for Issue/PR queue ownership.

validations.title: Slice planning review
criteria: The slice is the smallest useful change inside the active phase.
The task card includes goal, architecture context, acceptance criteria, tests to write first, non-goals, documentation updates, and verification commands.
Only one implementation direction is active; no unplanned refactors or speculative abstractions are included.

blocks.markdown: Plan `{{ task.input.sliceName }}` for phase `{{ task.input.phase }}`: {{ task.input.goal }}

items: Read docs/GOAL.md, docs/GLOSSARY.md, docs/DEVELOPMENT_WORKFLOW.md, docs/PHASED_DELIVERY_PLAN.md, docs/AGENT_SKILLS.md, docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, and relevant ADRs.
Define the smallest useful slice and prefer creating or updating the GitHub Issue task card when Issue work is in scope.
Identify relevant ADRs, glossary terms, architecture boundaries, non-goals, and docs updates.
Require tests or a test plan before implementation starts and keep implementation direction singular.

Preserve Resource as event-sourced aggregate root, one-Resource Action targets, one-Resource Event streams, declared External Operations, tests before implementation, docs as done, and no unplanned refactors.
